desc: Command: Extends a selected pipe upwards by radius × 5.
summary: Command: Extends a selected pipe upwards by radius × 5. Uses ExtendPipeUp for core extension logic. WORKFLOW: 1. User selects a pipe 2. Command gets pipe properties (system, size, routing type) 3. Delegates to ExtendPipeUp.Extend for the extension 4. Reports results to user via CommandFeedback
usecase: User needs to extend an SVP or drainage pipe upwards. User wants to quickly add a vent extension to existing pipework. User is extending vertical stacks through multiple floors.
notfor: Not for autonomous AI execution. This command requires interactive Revit UI input (dialogs, picks, or manual confirmation).
precondition: Active document must be open. At least one pipe must exist in the model. User must select a valid pipe element.
postcondition: A new pipe is created extending upwards from the top of the selected pipe. Extension length = pipe radius × 5. New pipe matches system, size and family routing types.
sideeffects: Creates a new pipe element in the model.
logictree: check: document.isOpen → continueaction: promptUserToSelectPipe → getSelectedPipecheck: selectedElement.isPipe → continue, else → erroraction: getPipeProperties → system, size, routingaction: calculateExtensionLength → radius × 5action: createExtensionPipe → ExtendPipeUp.Extendresolve: success → showSuccess, else → showError

selection_inputs.name: Pipe
selection_inputs.prompt: Select a pipe to extend upwards

config_inputs.name: RadiusMultiplier
config_inputs.description: Extension length as multiplier of radius

preprompts: Getting ready to extend the pipe...
